questions.question: During routine procurement under financial controls operations, which approach most strongly supports accountable implementation' (Financial control discipline scenario 1)
options: Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Delay escalation until issues become material and difficult to reverse.
questions.explanation: Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.

questions.question: A supervisor is reviewing gaps in procurement under financial controls. Which option best strengthens control and consistency' (Financial control discipline scenario 1)
options: Prioritize convenience over approved process requirements.
Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.
Bypass review checkpoints where timelines are tight.
Use inconsistent criteria across similar cases in the same period.
questions.explanation: Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.

questions.question: When decisions are made in procurement under financial controls, which step most directly improves traceability and fairness' (Financial control discipline scenario 1)
options: Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Rely on verbal approval and close the file without documentary trail.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.
questions.explanation: Commit spending only with authorization and complete supporting records.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines one of the key responsibilities of Accounting Officers under the Public Procurement Act?
options: To approve all payments without review.
To secure adequate appropriation is available for Procurements in their annual budget.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure adequate appropriation is available for Procurements in their annual budget.). Financial Regulation 113(b) states: "guarantee that adequate appropriation is available for Procurements in their annual budget."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: The Due workflow policy is described as a novel anti-corruption mechanism of the Federal Government aimed at providing transparency and accountability in what specific areas?
options: Judicial processes and legal frameworks a 385
Public sector procurement, government budgetary, and financial operations
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Public sector procurement, government budgetary, and financial operations). The Due procedure policy's aim is to provide transparency and accountability in procurement and in government budgetary and financial operations.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: The concept of "Due procedure" applies to which of the following aspects of government business?
options: All aspects of Government business, including negotiation, recruitment, and promotion
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (All aspects of Government business, including negotiation, recruitment, and promotion). A common error is associating "due procedure" only with procurement, but it applies to all other aspects of government business, such as negotiation, recruitment, and promotion.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, which role is accountable for causing surprise inspections to be carried out on the accounts of officers with financial responsibilities?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 126 states, "The Accountant-General must from time to time cause surprise inspections to be carried out on the accounts of any of the officers referred to in Regulation 123." Part 2: Financial Regulations: "Revenue Collection and Accounting,"
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which role is accountable for the overall supervision of the disbursement of funds and the proper monitoring and accounting for revenue?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry). Financial Regulation 114(b) states that one of the duties of the Head of Finance and Accounts is to guarantee "adequate supervision of the disbursement of funds and proper monitoring and accounting for revenue."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option best describes one of the main objectives of the Due workflow policy?
options: To increase government spending on all projects
To promote extra-budgetary spending in Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs)
To centralize all procurement decisions at the federal level a.
To strictly enforce due workflow principles of transparency, competition, and efficiency
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To strictly enforce due workflow principles of transparency, competition, and efficiency). A main objective is to strictly enforce due procedure principles of transparency, competition, efficiency, and value for money in the procurement of public goods, works, and services.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: responsibility for is accountable for approving and ensuring compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides, and stock verification manuals belongs to which role?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 107(i) states the Accountant-General must "approve and guarantee compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: If an Accounting Officer fails to comply with the provisions of the Public Procurement Act, who must be held personally liable for any breach or contravention?
options: Only the subordinates who caused the breach.
The Accounting Officer, whether or not caused by them in person or their subordinates.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accounting Officer, whether or not caused by them in person or their subordinates.). Financial Regulation 113(i) states the Accounting Officer "must be personally liable for any breach or contravention thereof, whether or not such breach or contravention was caused by them in person, their subordinates, or any person to whom they may have delegated their responsibilities."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the principal role of the Financial Regulations issued by the Minister of Finance?
options: To guide public officers on procurement only.
To guide public officers on the receipts and disbursements of Public Funds, and the management of public assets.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To guide public officers on the receipts and disbursements of Public Funds, and the management of public assets.). Financial Regulation 501 states: "Specifically, the Minister of Finance must from time to time direct the issuance of the Financial Regulations to guide all public officers on the receipts and disbursements of Public Funds, as well as the management of public assets."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: To whom should the approved Accounting Code, Internal Audit Guide, and Treasury Circulars be circulated within a ministry or self-accounting unit?
options: To all officers within the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of government for their guidance and compliance.
Only to the internal audit unit. □
Only to the Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To all officers within the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of government for their guidance and compliance.). Financial Regulation 507 states: "The approved Accounting Code and Internal Audit Guide of ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government or self accounting unit as well as Treasury circulars issued by the Accountant-General must be circulated to all officers within the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of government for their guidance and compliance."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What type of audit secure that laid down procedures are being observed in tendering, contracts, and store-keeping to prevent waste, pilferage, and extravagance?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Financial Control Audit.). Financial Regulation 109(l)(c) states: "Financial Control Audit-to guarantee that laid down procedures are being observed in tendering, contracts and store-keeping with a view to preventing waste, pilferage and Extravagance."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: What should be done with any balance of salary or other moneys due to an officer who has been dismissed for misappropriation of government funds?
options: It must not be paid without the authority of the Accounting Officer.
It should be held indefinitely by the government.
It should be paid immediately to the officer's family.
It must be used to disbursement off their debts. ¡3
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It must not be paid without the authority of the Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 1511 states that any balance of salary or other money due to an officer who has been convicted or dismissed for misappropriation or theft must not be paid without the Accounting Officer's authority.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes one of the key responsibilities of the Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit, as per Financial Regulation 1603(a)?
options: To personally conduct all internal audits.
To secure strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations.). Financial Regulation 1603(a) mandates that the Accounting Officer must secure strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations governing the receipt and disbursement of public funds.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option best describes a core element of proper financial management in a self-accounting unit that the Accounting Officer must secure?
options: Transparency and accountability in financial operations.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Transparency and accountability in financial operations.). The regulations emphasize strict observance of rules, proper accounting, and budget performance reporting, all pointing to transparency and accountability.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: What document outlines the responsibilities of an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Financial Regulations book, Chapter.). Chapter 16 of the Financial Regulations book is dedicated to "Self Accounting Status" and outlines these responsibilities..
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The Due procedure policy aims to prevent extra-budgetary spending in MDAs by ensuring what?
options: All MDAs can spend as much as they want
Projects are funded even without a budget □.
Funds are sourced from international donors only
Only projects with due appropriation by the National Assembly are certified and funded
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Only projects with due appropriation by the National Assembly are certified and funded). The policy aims to prevent extra-budgetary spending by ensuring that only 392 projects with due appropriation by the National Assembly are certified and funded for execution.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: responsibility for is the regulatory authority in charge for setting standards and developing the legal framework for public procurement in Nigeria belongs to which role?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Bureau of Public Procurement). The Bureau of Public Procurement is one of the regulatory authorities in charge for monitoring and oversight, and for setting standards and developing the legal framework for public procurement.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the option that an objective of the Due procedure policy related to project conceptualization?
options: To secure that project conceptualization and packaging match the defined priorities and targets as contained in the annual financial appropriations
To secure projects are disconnected from the annual financial appropriations
To allow project conceptualization to be done by external consultants only Q 393
To guarantee projects are conceptualized on an ad-hoc basis
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To secure that project conceptualization and packaging match the defined priorities and targets as contained in the annual financial appropriations). A key objective is to secure that project conceptualization and packaging match the defined priorities and targets as contained in the annual financial appropriations.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What does the term "Accounting Officer" mean?
options: The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or head of an extra-ministerial office is in charge for human, material, and financial resources.
An officer who keeps a cash book.3
The Chief Executive Officer of a parastatal.
Any officer involved in financial transactions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or head of an extra-ministerial office is in charge for human, material, and financial resources.). Financial Regulation 111 (i) defines "Accounting Officer" as "the Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of extra-ministerial office and other arms of government who is in full control of, and is accountable for, human, material, and financial resources." 151
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes one of the duties of the Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry?
options: To conduct external audits of the ministry.Q
To secure compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by staff.
To directly disburse all public funds.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To secure compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by staff.). Financial Regulation 114(a) states the Head of Finance and Accounts is in charge for "ensuring compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by all staff under his control and supervision."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that NOT included in the definition of a "Sub-Accounting Officer"?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Minister of a Ministry.). Financial Regulation 115(ii) lists various officers included, but the "Minister" is the Political Head (FR 116), not a Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What kind of checks should the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer use to supplement routine checks on Revenue Collectors' cash books?
options: Surprise inspections at the offices concerned
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Surprise inspections at the offices concerned). Financial Regulation 211 states that the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer must supplement routine checks with surprise inspections at the offices.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of necessitate for the abandonment of claims in the case of irrecoverable arrears of revenue?
options: The approval of the Accounting Officer.
The approval of the Head of Finance and Accounts.
The authority of the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The authority of the Minister of Finance.). Financial Regulation 231 (i) states that the authority of the Minister of Finance is necessitate for any abandonment of claims, except where other authority is established by law.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the limit of authority conveyed by Recurrent Expenditure Warrants to officers controlling votes?
options: Subject to the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
Limited to the amounts provided under each sub-head in the Approved Estimates and Supplementary Estimates.
Unlimited, as long as funds are available.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Limited to the amounts provided under each sub-head in the Approved Estimates and Supplementary Estimates.). Financial Regulation 313 states: "The authority conveyed... is limited to the amounts provided under each sub-head in the Approved Estimates and Supplementary Estimates."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which role is primarily in charge for making disbursements of public funds?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General and Accounting Officers.). Financial Regulation 401 (i) states: "All disbursements of public funds must be made by the Accountant General on the authority of the appropriate Warrant issued to him and also by Accounting Officers entrusted with the expenditure of public fund falling within the votes of charge of their ministries or extra ministerial offices and other arms, of government."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

subcategories.name: Audits, Sanctions & Compliance
subcategories.description: Audit controls, sanctions, and compliance obligations.

questions.question: The term **Audit Trail** refers to the sequential documentation of financial transactions to ensure:
options: Integrity and traceability, thereby ensuring accountability and transparency.
questions.explanation: Audit Trail refers to the sequential documentation of transactions to ensure integrity, traceability, accountability, and transparency Reference: Audits – Audit Trail.

questions.question: The type of internal control function that involves checking financial commitment before funds are paid, confirming legality and budgetary provision, is known as:
questions.explanation: Pre-audit functions involve checks before financial commitment is finalized to confirm legality and budgetary provision Reference: Audits – Types.

questions.question: The loss of government funds or property must be communicated immediately to:
options: The Head of the Civil Service and the Minister of Justice.
The Auditor-General of the Federation and the Accountant-General of the Federation.
The Minister of Finance and the National Assembly.
The officer's immediate supervisor and the Head of Accounts.
questions.explanation: Loss of government funds or property must be communicated immediately to the Auditor-General and the Accountant-General Reference: Audits – Loss Reporting.

questions.question: The maximum penalty for financial misconduct or fraud under the Financial Regulations may include:
questions.explanation: Public officers guilty of financial misconduct may face disciplinary action or prosecution, including surcharge and dismissal Reference: Sanctions – Penalties.

questions.question: The primary anti-corruption agency responsible for investigating and prosecuting financial crimes like fraud and money laundering is the:
questions.explanation: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) investigates and prosecutes financial crimes, fraud, and money laundering Reference: Sanctions – Anti-Corruption.

questions.question: Which body monitors compliance with anti-corruption laws, focuses on institutional corruption, and enforces ethical standards in public finance
questions.explanation: The Independent Corrupt Practices Commission (ICPC) focuses on institutional corruption and enforcement of ethical standards Reference: Sanctions – Anti-Corruption.

questions.question: Dismissal of an officer for serious financial misconduct must be supported by:
options: A direct memo from the Minister.
A Board of Inquiry report and internal investigation findings.
questions.explanation: Dismissal for financial misconduct must be supported by internal audit investigation findings and often requires a Board of Inquiry following due process Reference: Sanctions – Disciplinary Procedure.

questions.question: The concept of organizing financial tasks such that no single individual controls all stages of a transaction, thereby preventing fraud, is known as:
questions.explanation: Segregation of Duties is a crucial internal control mechanism designed to prevent fraud by distributing control over financial operations Reference: Audits – Internal Controls.

questions.question: Which concept describes mechanisms put in place to trace financial transactions and detect irregularities
questions.explanation: The Audit Trail documents the flow of transactions, and the Internal Audit monitors compliance to detect irregularities Reference: Audits – Control Mechanisms.

questions.question: The penalty for unauthorized expenditure, known as **Excess Vote**, is typically identified through:
options: The Auditor-General's Report on public accounts.
questions.explanation: The Auditor-General reports on all unauthorized or excess expenditure in their report on public accounts Reference: Audits – Reporting Excess.

questions.question: The use of government funds beyond the budget without subsequent legislative approval is classified as:
questions.explanation: Unauthorized expenditure is categorized as misappropriation or unauthorized spending Reference: Sanctions – Misappropriation.

questions.question: The final authority to restore a deferred increment, which requires assessing service improvement and conduct, lies with the:
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment (Rule 040208).

questions.question: If an officer causes financial loss, the penalty imposed (surcharge) is intended to recover:
options: The exact amount of financial loss caused by the officer's fault.
A fixed statutory amount unrelated to the loss.
questions.explanation: Surcharge is the recovery of government losses from the officer's pay, applied when the loss is due to the officer’s fault Reference: Sanctions – Surcharge Recovery.

questions.question: Which of the following bodies is empowered to grant special increments to an officer whose increment was previously withheld
options: The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC) is the body empowered to grant special increments Reference: Sanctions – Special Increment Authority.

questions.question: The ultimate disciplinary penalty for serious misconduct, such as fraud or corruption, resulting in the forfeiture of all claims to retirement benefits, is:
questions.explanation: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct is dismissal, resulting in the forfeiture of benefits, subject to the Pension Reform Act (Rule 100407) [142-144].

questions.question: Which officer's responsibility includes advising the Accounting Officer on the adequacy and functioning of the internal controls within the **MDA**'
questions.explanation: The Head of Internal Audit is responsible for independently evaluating financial activities and controls and advising the Accounting Officer Reference: Audits – Internal Auditor Role.

questions.question: The purpose of activating an **Audit Alarm** is to necessitate:
options: A detailed investigation into the suspected irregularity.
Automatic transfer of the implicated officer.
The suspension of all staff salary payments.
questions.explanation: An Audit Alarm necessitates a detailed investigation into the suspected irregularity flagged immediately Reference: Audits – Audit Alarm Action.

questions.question: The term **'Accruals'** in government financial reporting refers to:
options: Cash received before services are delivered to beneficiaries.
Expenses or revenues recognized when incurred, not when cash is exchanged.
Budget stabilization reserves retained by the Ministry of Finance.
Statutory penalties imposed for delayed remittance of taxes.
questions.explanation: Accruals refer to expenses or revenues recognized when incurred, not when cash changes hands Reference: Audits – Financial Terminology.

questions.question: Which type of audit, mandated under the **PPA**, involves the review of a bidder's documentation to confirm eligibility after the bid opening
questions.explanation: Post-Qualification audit is the review of bidder’s documentation to confirm eligibility after bid opening Reference: Audits – Procurement Audit.

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts
options: Required to resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Suspension until they pass the exam.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to resign or be withdrawn from the service (Rule 030104).

questions.question: Which agency is responsible for enforcing ethical standards in public office, particularly by receiving and verifying public officers’ asset declarations
options: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC).
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) receives asset declarations and monitors compliance with ethical standards Reference: Sanctions – Ethics Enforcement.

questions.question: When issuing an Audit Query (**FR** 1204), the Accounting Officer must address the formal response to
questions.explanation: The Audit Query is a formal question from auditors, and the response must be addressed back to the originating authority (the issuing Auditor) for resolution Reference: Audits – Query Response.

questions.question: The Auditor-General's Report primarily focuses on the review of:
questions.explanation: The Audit Report focuses on the review of public accounts (past expenditure and revenue) and is critical for legislative oversight Reference: Audits – Legislative Review.

questions.question: To whom must an Officer promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge, under disciplinary action, whenever such event occurs
options: His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: An Officer must promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (Rule 100409).

questions.question: The disciplinary authority responsible for exercising control over Officers in the Federal Civil Service (**GL 07** and above) is vested in the:
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The power to dismiss and to exercise disciplinary control over Officers in the Federal Civil Service is vested in the Federal Civil Service Commission (Rule 100102).

questions.question: Which specific body has delegated full disciplinary powers to Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices in respect of officers on Salary **GL 13** and below
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The FCSC delegates disciplinary powers to Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices for officers on GL 13 and below [156-158] Reference: Sanctions – Delegated Powers.

questions.question: If the **FCSC** does not approve the Officer's dismissal or impose any penalty, the Officer shall be reinstated forthwith and be entitled to the full amount of salary denied him if he was interdicted or suspended, as stipulated by:
questions.explanation: If the Commission does not approve the Officer’s dismissal, he shall be reinstated forthwith and be entitled to the full amount of salary denied him if he was interdicted or suspended (Rule 100307(xi)).

questions.question: Which of the following acts constitutes **Gross Misconduct** under the Civil Service Handbook
questions.explanation: Fraud and falsification of records constitute gross misconduct in the civil service Reference: Sanctions – Gross Misconduct.

questions.question: The misconduct described as absenteeism without official permission is classified as:
questions.explanation: Absenteeism without permission is classified as gross misconduct Reference: Sanctions – Absenteeism.

questions.question: Which committee's recommendation forms the basis for the discipline (including removal from office) of officers on Salary **GL 06** and below by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
questions.explanation: Discipline and removal of Officers on GL 06 and below is based on the recommendation of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) (Rule 100104).

questions.question: Concealment of facts or any false statement made in an application for Government employment shall be regarded as sufficient grounds for:
options: Granting probation only, under the applicable service framework.
Non-engagement or subsequent termination of appointment without notice.
Reduction in rank, in line with formal public service practice.
Annual salary freeze, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Concealment of facts or any false statement in an application for employment shall be regarded as sufficient grounds for non-engagement or subsequent termination of appointment without notice (Rule 100431).

questions.question: Which type of assessment report is often used as evidence of **General Inefficiency** (**Rule 100201**)
options: Financial Audit Report, under the applicable service framework.
Poor performance appraisals, within established administrative procedures.
Ministerial Tenders Board Minutes, in line with formal public service practice.
Security Clearance Report, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: General inefficiency is directly linked to performance and competence, captured in performance appraisals (Chapter 5).

questions.question: Before documentary evidence can be used against an officer during an inquiry (**Rule 100307(vii**)), the officer must previously have been provided with a copy thereof or given:
questions.explanation: No documentary evidence shall be used against the Officer unless he has previously been supplied with a copy thereof or given open access thereto (Rule 100307(vii)).

questions.question: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct, resulting in the forfeiture of all claims to retirement benefits, leave, or transport grant, is subject to the provisions of the:
questions.explanation: Dismissal results in forfeiture subject to the provisions of the Pension Reform Act, 2014 (as amended) (Rule 100407).

questions.question: The disciplinary process starts with a formal query issued by which officer
options: The Minister, under the applicable service framework.
The Auditor-General, in line with formal public service practice.
A Colleague, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The disciplinary process starts with a query issued by the supervisor or Head of Department (Rule 100307(i)).

questions.question: An officer involved in procurement fraud, such as collusion or over-invoicing, faces which penalty under the Public Procurement Act (**PPA**) framework (**Section 58**)
options: Dismissal or criminal charges (imprisonment and fines).
questions.explanation: Section 58 prescribes imprisonment and fines for procurement-related fraud, which constitutes gross misconduct leading to dismissal.

questions.question: Which of the following disciplinary penalties is generally **prohibited** under the Financial Regulations (**FR** 3401)
questions.explanation: Disciplinary penalties may include surcharges, reductions, or warnings, but imposing monetary fines on officers as a penalty is generally prohibited Reference: Sanctions – Prohibited Penalties.

questions.question: The expression 'serious financial embarrassment' (**Rule 100414**) refers to an officer's state of indebtedness which has actually caused serious financial hardship to him and which may be prejudicial to:
options: The general meaning of the said expression.
The ability of the government to pay him.
questions.explanation: Serious financial embarrassment relates to indebtedness causing hardship prejudicial to the general meaning of the expression (Rule 100414).

questions.question: If an officer is suspected of **General Inefficiency**, the superior officer must first administer instruction and warning, and give the officer adequate time to redeem his image, for the objective of improving the officer's:
questions.explanation: The objective is to improve the officer's usefulness and efficiency (Rule 100201 practice).

questions.question: The minimum period for which an increment deferment must be fixed is:
questions.explanation: If an increment is deferred, the period must be fixed for not less than six months (Rule 040205).

questions.question: Which of the following disciplinary measures can be taken against a Permanent Secretary found guilty of misconduct (**Rule 100702**)
questions.explanation: Disciplinary measures against a Permanent Secretary include dismissal, termination, or retirement (Rule 100702).

questions.question: An officer, upon resumption from casual or special leave within the country, provided he spends such leave in Nigeria, is NOT required to have whom appointed to act in the duty post in question
questions.explanation: An acting Officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of his acting appointment by proceeding on casual or special leave within the country, and it is not necessary during any such leave to appoint another Officer to act in the duty post in question (Rule 020708(a)).

questions.question: The dismissal of Officers on **GL 06** and below requires the ultimate approval of which body, despite the involvement of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC)
questions.explanation: The dismissal of Officers on GL 06 and below is an exception to the delegated powers and still requires the approval of the FCSC (Rule 100103).

questions.question: If an officer resigns from the Service, they will forfeit all claims to vacation leave, and any leave privileges granted will be:
questions.explanation: An Officer who resigns will forfeit claims to vacation leave, and any leave privileges granted will be ex-gratia (Rule 020904(i)).

questions.question: Accepting paid employment during a period of Leave of Absence without the express approval of the **OHCSF** may lead to which disciplinary consequence
questions.explanation: Accepting paid employment without approval (Rule 100429) constitutes misconduct and can lead to severe disciplinary action, including dismissal.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered 'serious misconduct' under the **PSR** (**Rule 100402**)
questions.explanation: Falsification of records is listed explicitly as an act of serious misconduct (Rule 100402(a)).

questions.question: In the case of an inquiry into serious misconduct, the head of the officer’s department shall generally **not** be a member of the inquiry board, in order to maintain:
questions.explanation: The head of the officer's department shall not be a member of the board (Rule 100307(v)) to ensure impartiality in the disciplinary process.

questions.question: An officer convicted of a criminal offence (other than a minor traffic or sanitary offence) shall be placed under suspension with effect from the date of conviction, pending determination of his case by the Commission, as per:
questions.explanation: An officer convicted of a criminal offence shall be suspended pending the Commission's determination (Rule 100412).

questions.question: An officer absent from duty without leave (**Rule 100413**) renders himself liable to be dismissed from the Service, and the onus rests entirely on him to show that circumstances do not justify the imposition of the full penalty, due to the concept of:
questions.explanation: Absence without leave where the onus rests on the officer to justify the absence implies desertion or serious misconduct (Rule 100413).

questions.question: What is the penalty for unauthorized absence from duty
options: Loss of pay for the period of absence.
questions.explanation: Unauthorized absence from duty results in loss of pay for the period of absence (Rule 040206).

questions.question: In the context of Audits, Sanctions & Compliance, which action best demonstrates audits, sanctions & compliance governance
options: Apply approved audits, sanctions & compliance procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved audits, sanctions & compliance procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in audits, sanctions & compliance Reference: Audits, Sanctions & Compliance - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective audits, sanctions & compliance, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure audits, sanctions & compliance compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: To whom does the Internal Auditor submit periodic audit reports?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 1706 explicitly states that periodic audit reports are submitted to the Accounting Officer.

questions.question: Within government administration, responsibility for receives the three copies of the Board of Survey report belongs to which role?
options: The Accounting Officer, the Accountant-General, and the Auditor-General.
The Auditor-General, the Head of Internal Audit, and the Head of Department
The Accounting Officer, the Head of Department, and the officer-in-charge.
The Accounting Officer, the Head of the Internal Audit Unit, and the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accounting Officer, the Accountant-General, and the Auditor-General.). Financial Regulation 1807 directs that one copy of the report goes to the Accounting Officer, one to the Accountant-General, and one to the Auditor-General.

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What happens if an Internal Auditor discovers that an officer is negligently disregarding Financial Regulations?
options: The Internal Auditor should ignore it if it is a small error.Q
The Internal Auditor should inform the officer's supervisor.
The Internal Auditor should communicate his discovery to the Accounting Officer in a "management letter." 275
The Internal Auditor should write a note to the Auditor-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Internal Auditor should communicate his discovery to the Accounting Officer in a "management letter." 275). Financial Regulation 1703(e) necessitate the Internal Auditor to communicate such discoveries to the Accounting Officer in a "management letter."

questions.question: Within government administration, which office is in charge for the proper custody of public funds, stamps, and other government documents in a Ministry, Department, or Agency (MDA)?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Director of Finance and Accounts.). The Director of Finance and Accounts is in charge for the proper custody of all public funds, stamps, and other government documents.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What must Accounting Officers do upon the establishment of any new revenue collecting office?
options: Notify the Central Bank of Nigeriaa
Notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General
Notify the Head of Internal Audit
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General). Financial Regulation 217 specifies that Accounting Officers will notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General of the establishment of any new revenue-collecting office.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the consequence if a loss of public fund is revealed following an investigation into a suspected irregularity?
options: The officer will be given a warning.
The loss will be covered by the Contingencies Fund.
Action must be taken in accordance with Chapter 25 of these Regulations.
The matter will be referred to the National Assembly. 193 Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Action must be taken in accordance with Chapter 25 of these Regulations.). Financial Regulation 409 states: "Where a loss of 'public fund is revealed, action must be taken in accordance with Chapter 25 of these Regulations."

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: What documents are federal ministries and other arms of government necessitate to use for their daily financial transactions?
options: The Financial Regulations, Treasury Accounting Manual, and Treasury Circulars issued by the Office of the Accountant General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Financial Regulations, Treasury Accounting Manual, and Treasury Circulars issued by the Office of the Accountant General.). Financial Regulation 503 states: "For this objective, federal ministries/extra ministerial offices and other arms of government must use the Financial Regulations, Treasury Accounting Manual and Treasury Circulars issued by the Office of the Accountant General in accounting for their daily financial transactions."

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the definition of an "Accounting Officer" in the context of these regulations? 200?
options: The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of an extra-ministerial office who is in full control of and accountable for human, material, and financial resources.
The officer in charge for internal audit.
The officer who signs disbursement vouchers
Any officer who handles public money.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of an extra-ministerial office who is in full control of and accountable for human, material, and financial resources.). Financial Regulation 111 (i) states: "The Term 'Accounting Officer' means the Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of extra-ministerial office and other arms of government who is in full control of, and is in charge for human, material and financial resources which are critical inputs' in the management of an organization."

questions.question: According to established rules, which role is personally accountable for ensuring that the balance of cash with the cashier is locked in a strong-room or safe at the close of business daily?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Sub-Accounting Officers.). Financial Regulation 1120 assigns this accountability to "Sub-Accounting Officers."

questions.question: According to established rules, which role is in charge for ensuring that all stores are physically secure?
options: The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.). Financial Regulation 2128(b) places the duty for the physical security of all government stores on the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the aim of the audit query issued by the Auditor-General for the Federation?
options: To highlight any financial anomalies, unauthorized expenditures, or lack of proper accounting
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To highlight any financial anomalies, unauthorized expenditures, or lack of proper accounting). The Auditor-General's report is based on audit queries, which highlight financial 396 irregularities such as lack of proper accounting, unauthorized expenditures, and other financial anomalies.

questions.question: In official practice, select the statement that correctly defines the accountability of the Political Head regarding his ministry, agency, and/or parastatal?
options: To manage its day-to-day financial transactions.
To supervise and control its activities.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To supervise and control its activities.). Financial Regulation 118(i) states, "It must be the duty of the Political Head to supervise and control the activities of his ministry, agency, and/or parastatal."

questions.question: In the public service context, responsibility for is in charge for checking Revenue Collectors' cash books and cash balances at irregular intervals belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accounting Officer). Financial Regulation 217 states that the Accounting Officer may consider it necessary for a revenue collecting office to have its cash books and cash balances checked at irregular intervals.

questions.question: In official practice, which role is in charge for ensuring that provisions for Value Added Tax (VAT) and Withholding Tax (WHT) are made and remitted?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Accounting Officers). Financial Regulation 234(i) makes it mandatory for Accounting Officers to guarantee full compliance with the dual roles of making provision for and remitting VAT and WHT

questions.question: Within government administration, which office is in charge for verifying the Head and subhead inserted by the Revenue Collector on the paying-in form?
options: The Head of Internal Audit 166 Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The receiving cashier). Financial Regulation 209(i) states that the receiving cashier will, in each case, verify the Head and subhead.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the aim of the Revenue Collector's paying-in Form (Treasury Form 15)?
options: To track the movements of the Revenue Collector.
To serve as an internal audit document.
To support the lodgement of moneys with a Sub-Accounting Officer.
To report a loss of funds. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To support the lodgement of moneys with a Sub-Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 218 mentions that a lodgement with a Sub-Accounting Officer will be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15).

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the chief accountability of the Political Head of a Ministry regarding its activities?
options: To supervise and control its activities.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To supervise and control its activities.). Financial Regulation 118(i) states: "It must be the accountability of the Political Head to supervise and control the activities of his ministry, agency and/or parastatal."

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines one of the duties of the Accountant-General regarding accounting codes and manuals?
options: To formulate them without external input.
To approve and guarantee compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals.
To distribute them to the public. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To approve and guarantee compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals.). Financial Regulation 107(i) states: "approve and secure compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals of federal ministries, extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."

questions.question: choose the best answer for: If a key holder suspects interference with the keys or locks of a strong-room or safe, what must they do?
options: Ignore it unless a loss occurs.
Remove the contents to another safe, report the circumstances, and request immediate steps to alter the locks and provide new keys.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Remove the contents to another safe, report the circumstances, and request immediate steps to alter the locks and provide new keys.). Financial Regulation 1113 directs the key holder to "remove the contents to another safe, report the circumstances as directed in Regulation 1111, and ask that immediate steps be taken to have the locks altered and new keys provided."

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes a key duty of the Internal Auditor as per Financial Regulation 1703?
options: To audit and certify all disbursement vouchers.
To conduct an annual audit of the entire Ministry.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To audit and certify all disbursement vouchers.). Financial Regulation 1703(d) lists one of the duties as auditing and certifying all payment vouchers.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the Internal Auditor's guide for auditing procedures known as, according to FR 1708?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (An Audit Guide.). Financial Regulation 1708 states that the Head of Internal Audit Unit must prepare and issue an "Audit Guide" for the use of audit staff.

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What must an Internal Auditor do when he discovers an apparent abuse of Financial Regulations?
options: Call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing.
Take disciplinary action against the officer. □
Report it immediately to the Accountant-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing.). Financial Regulation 1703(a) states the Internal Auditor must call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing where there is any apparent abuse of Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of necessitate for the issuance of audit stamps to Internal Audit staff?
options: A form signed by the Accountant-General.
Verbal permission from the Head of Department.
A requisition form (Treasury Form 65A) in triplicate. a
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A requisition form (Treasury Form 65A) in triplicate. a). Financial Regulation 1711 details the procedure for issuing audit stamps, requiring a requisition form (Treasury Form 65A) in triplicate.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the significance of the Subsidiary Account Pension Unit functioning independently?
options: It secure specialization and clear accountability for pension-related financial procedures.
It simplifies the overall accounting procedure
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It secure specialization and clear accountability for pension-related financial procedures.). The independent functioning, as per FR 1905, suggests a focus on specialized and accountable management of pension funds.

questions.question: which office is accountable for examining the logbook regularly and signing against the entries?
options: The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.). Financial Regulation 2007 states that the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office must examine the logbook regularly and sign against the entries.

subcategories.name: General Financial Management
subcategories.description: TSA, reconciliation, statements, and core PFM principles.

questions.question: Who is designated as the Chief Accounting Officer personally responsible for the control, custody, and disbursement of funds within a Ministry or Extra-Ministerial Department (**MDA**)
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary is the Chief Accounting Officer of a Ministry, personally responsible for all financial operations and integrity Reference: General Financial Management – Accounting Officer.

questions.question: Which government official is responsible for issuing **Treasury Circulars** that provide detailed instructions on financial procedures and implementation across **MDAs**'
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General of the Federation issues Treasury Circulars to guide accounting officers on procedures [179-181] Reference: General Financial Management – Authority.

questions.question: Which officer is primarily responsible for maintaining the departmental **Vote Book** to monitor budgetary allocations and expenditures against commitments
questions.explanation: The Vote Controller maintains the Vote Book (or departmental vote ledger) to monitor budgetary allocations and expenditures Reference: General Financial Management – Vote Book.

questions.question: The main purpose of the **Cash Book** is:
options: To monitor inflow and outflow of cash.
questions.explanation: The Cash Book is used to record all inflows and outflows of cash, supporting reconciliation and control Reference: General Financial Management – Record Keeping.

questions.question: Which agency is responsible for managing Nigeria’s public debt, covering both domestic and external obligations
questions.explanation: The Debt Management Office (DMO) is responsible for managing Nigeria’s public debt Reference: General Financial Management – DMO.

questions.question: What does **TSA** stand for in Nigeria’s financial system
questions.explanation: TSA stands for Treasury Single Account, which consolidates government revenues into a single account for transparency Reference: General Financial Management – TSA.

questions.question: Which law provides the legal framework for prudent management of public finances in Nigeria
questions.explanation: The Fiscal Responsibility Act provides the legal framework for prudent management of public finances in Nigeria.

questions.question: In the public service context, which office permit payments from the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF)?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The National Assembly.). Payments from the Consolidated Revenue Fund must be empower by the National Assembly Reference: General Financial Management – CRF Authority.

questions.question: Which document accompanies all payments made in Ministries, detailing the expenditure and authorization
questions.explanation: All government payments must be backed by duly approved Payment Vouchers Reference: General Financial Management – Payment Document.

questions.question: What is the **Consolidated Revenue Fund**'
options: Account for capital projects, under the applicable service framework.
Main account into which all government revenues are paid.
Account for external loans, in line with formal public service practice.
Account for emergency funds, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) is the main account into which all government revenues are paid Reference: General Financial Management – CRF Definition.

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring that all government revenue is remitted into the Treasury Single Account (**TSA**)
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General ensures that all revenues are remitted into the Treasury Single Account Reference: General Financial Management – TSA Compliance.

questions.question: Which specific Nigerian legal instrument established the Office of the Auditor-General for the Federation (AuGF)
questions.explanation: The 1999 Constitution of Nigeria established the Office of the Auditor-General for the Federation Reference: General Financial Management – AuGF Establishment.

questions.question: What is the purpose of issuing a **Treasury Receipt**'
questions.explanation: A Treasury Receipt is issued to acknowledge payments made into government coffers (revenue collection) Reference: General Financial Management – Revenue Documentation.

questions.question: Which government fund is statutorily established to meet unforeseen and urgent government expenditures
questions.explanation: The Contingencies Fund is used to meet unforeseen and urgent government expenditures [53-55] Reference: General Financial Management – Contingencies Fund.

questions.question: What is the meaning of **'public expenditure'**'
options: All government spending from public funds.
questions.explanation: Public expenditure includes all government spending from public funds on recurrent and capital projects Reference: General Financial Management – Expenditure Definition.

questions.question: What does **'Recurrent Expenditure'** include
questions.explanation: Recurrent Expenditure covers spending that is regular and occurs within the fiscal year, such as salaries and overhead costs Reference: General Financial Management – Recurrent Expenditure.

questions.question: The **Contingency Fund** is reserved for which type of expenditure
questions.explanation: The Contingency Fund is reserved for emergency and unforeseen government expenditures Reference: General Financial Management – Contingency Fund Purpose.
questions.chapter: General Financial Management – Contingency Fund Purpose

questions.question: Who is the principal signatory to government bank accounts
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General is the principal signatory to government bank accounts, controlling federal receipts and payments Reference: General Financial Management – AGF Role.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for certifying that services have been duly performed before payment is made to a vendor
questions.explanation: The Head of Department certifies that services have been duly performed before payments are made Reference: General Financial Management – Certification.

questions.question: What does the term **'accruals'** mean in government financial reporting
options: Cash received in advance for services, under the applicable service framework.
Expenses or revenues recognized when incurred, not when cash is exchanged.
Budgetary reserves held by the Minister of Finance.
Penalties imposed for late payment of taxes.
questions.explanation: Accruals refer to expenses or revenues recognized when incurred, not when cash changes hands Reference: General Financial Management – Accruals.

questions.question: Which agency regulates Nigeria’s capital market
questions.explanation: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regulates Nigeria’s capital market Reference: General Financial Management – Regulation.

questions.question: What does **IGR** stand for in public finance
questions.explanation: IGR stands for Internally Generated Revenue, which refers to income generated by states or ministries apart from federal allocations Reference: General Financial Management – IGR.

questions.question: Who is responsible for issuing receipts for government revenue upon collection
questions.explanation: Revenue Collectors are responsible for issuing receipts for all government revenues received Reference: General Financial Management – Revenue Collection.

questions.question: Which specific account holds all federal revenues before distribution among the three tiers of government
questions.explanation: The Federation Account holds revenues before distribution among the three tiers of government Reference: General Financial Management – Federation Account.

questions.question: Which account is historically used for financing capital projects in Nigeria
questions.explanation: The Development Fund is used for financing capital projects in Nigeria Reference: General Financial Management – Development Fund.

questions.question: Which dedicated account is used for repayment of Nigeria’s external and domestic debts
questions.explanation: The Debt Service Account is used for repayment of Nigeria’s external and domestic debts Reference: General Financial Management – Debt Account.

questions.question: Which Nigerian law established the Debt Management Office (DMO)
questions.explanation: The Debt Management Office was established by the Debt Management Act 2003.

questions.question: What is the primary responsibility of the **Treasury** in Nigeria
options: To manage government cash flow and payments.
questions.explanation: The Treasury manages government cash flows, payments, and receipts Reference: General Financial Management – Treasury Role.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the **Accountant-General of the Federation**'
options: To regulate banks, under the applicable service framework.
To manage and control all receipts and payments of the federal government.
To conduct audits, in line with formal public service practice.
To manage procurement, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General manages and controls all receipts and payments of the federal government Reference: General Financial Management – AGF Primary Role.
questions.chapter: General Financial Management – AGF Primary Role

questions.question: Which Nigerian agency regulates and supervises banks
questions.explanation: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) regulates and supervises banks in Nigeria Reference: General Financial Management – Banking Regulation.

questions.question: What is the main source of Nigeria’s Federation Account revenue
questions.explanation: Oil revenue remains the primary source of Nigeria’s Federation Account revenue Reference: General Financial Management – Revenue Source.

questions.question: What is the main source of Local Government revenue
questions.explanation: The main source of Local Government revenue is allocations from the Federation Account Reference: General Financial Management – LG Revenue.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Fiscal Responsibility Act
options: To promote transparency in public finance.
questions.explanation: The Fiscal Responsibility Act promotes transparency, accountability, and prudence in the management of public finance.

questions.question: Which financial instrument is issued by the Federal Government to raise short-term funds in the money market
questions.explanation: Treasury Bills are short-term instruments issued by government to raise funds Reference: General Financial Management – Financial Instruments.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for preparing and maintaining the departmental **vote ledger**'
questions.explanation: The Vote Controller maintains the departmental vote ledger Reference: General Financial Management – Vote Ledger.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for preparing bank reconciliation statements monthly
questions.explanation: The Accounts Officer is responsible for preparing bank reconciliation statements Reference: General Financial Management – Accounting Duties.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a **Bank Reconciliation Statement**'
options: To calculate imprest, under the applicable service framework.
To reconcile differences between Cash Book and Bank Statement.
To approve loans, in line with formal public service practice.
To retire advances, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: A Bank Reconciliation Statement reconciles differences between the Cash Book and the Bank Statement Reference: General Financial Management – Reconciliation.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for maintaining the General Ledger for the entire Federal Government
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General of the Federation is responsible for maintaining the General Ledger Reference: General Financial Management – Ledger Management.

questions.question: Which officer certifies that funds are available in the budget before any expenditure or commitment is incurred
questions.explanation: The Vote Controller certifies that funds are available before incurring expenditure Reference: General Financial Management – Fund Certification.

questions.question: Which officer is statutorily designated as the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government
questions.explanation: The Minister of Finance is the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government Reference: General Financial Management – Chief Adviser.

questions.question: Which fund is established specifically for the replacement of depreciating government assets
questions.explanation: The Replacement Reserve Fund is used for replacing depreciating government assets [39-41] Reference: General Financial Management – Reserve Fund.

questions.question: What must **'public revenue'** include
options: Only oil revenue, under the applicable service framework.
Only taxes, within established administrative procedures.
All government income including taxes, oil, and non-oil sources.
Only levies, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Public revenue includes all government income, such as taxes, oil, customs, levies, and other sources Reference: General Financial Management – Revenue Scope.

questions.question: What is the meaning of **'Capital Expenditure'** in financial terms
options: Spending on acquiring or maintaining fixed assets.
questions.explanation: Capital Expenditure is spending on assets and infrastructure that create future benefits Reference: General Financial Management – Capital Definition.

questions.question: The **Government Integrated Financial Management Information System (**GIFMIS**)** is primarily designed to integrate:
options: All MDA financial operations, accounting, and expenditure tracking.
questions.explanation: GIFMIS supports integrated financial operations, accounting, and expenditure tracking across MDAs Reference: General Financial Management – GIFMIS.

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: The total amount of funds allocated for staff salaries (Personnel Emoluments) in the budget is controlled primarily by data sourced from which platform?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (IPPIS.). The Integrated Personnel and Payroll Information System (IPPIS) holds the accurate data on staff nominal roll, grade levels, and steps, which determines personnel emoluments [34-36] Reference: General Financial Management – Personnel Cost Control.
questions.chapter: General Financial Management – Personnel Cost Control

questions.question: Which officer prepares Nigeria’s annual budget performance reports for review
questions.explanation: The Budget Officer prepares annual budget performance reports for review Reference: General Financial Management – Reporting Officer.

questions.question: Which document is used to request for the release of funds from the Treasury to the **MDAs**'
questions.explanation: A Warrant provides for the release of funds for both capital and recurrent expenditures Reference: General Financial Management – Funds Release.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for certifying stock balances in government stores periodically
questions.explanation: The Store Officer certifies stock balances in government stores periodically Reference: General Financial Management – Stores Management.

questions.question: Which officer is responsible for certifying the physical existence and accuracy of stores received or periodically verifying stock balances in government stores
questions.explanation: The Store Officer is explicitly tasked with certifying the accuracy of stores received and certifying stock balances in government stores periodically, a fundamental aspect of inventory control and fraud prevention Reference: General Financial Management – Stores Management.

questions.question: The movement of funds from one specified budget subhead to another **within the same expenditure vote**, authorized by a Warrant, is known as:
questions.explanation: Virement is the authorized transfer of funds from one sub-head to another within the same head of expenditure Reference: FR Budgeting.

questions.question: The use of the Government Integrated Financial Management Information System (**GIFMIS**) primarily supports the financial management process by providing:
options: Integrated financial operations, accounting, and expenditure tracking.
The exclusive mechanism for collecting tax revenue.
A register of physical assets only.
questions.explanation: GIFMIS supports real-time public finance tracking, integrating budgeting, accounting, and expenditure Reference: FR Budgeting – GIFMIS.

questions.question: In the context of General Financial Management, which action best demonstrates general financial management governance
options: Apply approved general financial management procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved general financial management procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general financial management Reference: General Financial Management - Expansion Set.

options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in general financial management Reference: General Financial Management - Expansion Set.
