Validate authorization, records, and control checkpoints before action.
Use inconsistent criteria across similar cases.
questions.explanation: Validate authorization, records, and control checkpoints before action.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: To whom should requests for Treasury Receipts Books be submitted?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1205 states that requests for Treasury Receipts Books should be submitted to the Accountant-General.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: The Accountant-General is accountable for maintaining and operating the accounts of which major public funds?
options: Private bank accounts of public officers.a 147
Consolidated Revenue Fund, Development Fund, and Contingencies Fund.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Consolidated Revenue Fund, Development Fund, and Contingencies Fund.). Financial Regulation 107(e) states the Accountant-General maintains and operates accounts of the "Consolidated Revenue Fund, Development Fund, Contingencies Fund and Other Public Funds."

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: From which fund is Recurrent Expenditure primarily paid?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Consolidated Revenue Fund.). Financial Regulation 302(i) states: "Recurrent expenditure is paid from the Consolidated Revenue Fund..."

questions.question: select the option that best answers: Funds transferred from the Contingencies Fund by a Supplementary (Contingencies) Warrant are transferred to which fund?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Consolidated Revenue Fund.). Financial Regulation 306 states it empower the Accountant-General "to transfer the necessary funds from the Contingencies Fund to the Consolidated Revenue Fund."

questions.question: In the public service context, which office is in charge for ensuring the full collection of all government revenues and for promptly bringing them to account?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Accounting Officer Q). Financial Regulation 201 explicitly states that Accounting Officers are accountable for the full collection of all revenues due to the government and for their prompt lodgment and accounting.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option NOT a form of warrant by which the Minister of Finance conveys authority for recurrent expenditure?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Development Fund (Special) Warrant.). Financial Regulation 302(ii) lists the forms of warrants for recurrent expenditure. The Development Fund (Special) Warrant is for Capital Expenditure (FR 318).

questions.question: According to established rules, which office has the right of access to records of all bank accounts of all ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of government?
options: The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.
The Head of Finance and Accounts. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.). Financial Regulation 709 states: "The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives must have the right of access to records of all bank accounts of all ministries, extra ministerial offices and other arms of government..."

questions.question: In the public service context, which office issues officially approved forms bearing Treasury numbers for use in all federal ministries to secure uniformity?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 107(n) states the Accountant-General must "issue officially approved forms bearing Treasury numbers for use in all federal ministries... to guarantee uniformity."

questions.question: In the public service context, which role is accountable for issuing Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manuals to guide Accounting Officers and other employees?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.3
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 502 states: "Pursuant to FR 501, the Accountant-General must issue from time to time Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manual to guide Accounting Officers and other employees of each ministries/extra- ministerial offices and other arms of government 01) all matters relating to the provisions of the Financial Regulations."

questions.question: Within government administration, which role is in charge for supervising and controlling the computerization of the accounting system in federal ministries?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 107(1) states the Accountant-General must "supervise and control the computerization of the accounting system."

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What happens to an unspent balance on a recurrent expenditure vote at the end of the financial year?
options: It is transferred to the Development Fund.
It is returned to the Federation Account
It is carried forward to the next year.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (It lapses.). Financial Regulation 314 states, "An unspent balance on a recurrent, expenditure vote cannot be revoted." and FR 413(i) states "The authority for recurrent expenditure... lapses at the end of the financial year..."

questions.question: In official practice, which role has free access to books of accounts, files, safes, security documents, and other records relating to the accounts of all federal ministries?
options: The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.
The Head of Finance and Accounts. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.). Financial Regulation 110 states: "By virtue of the responsibilities and functions of the Accountant General and the Auditor-General or their representatives must, at all reasonable times, have free access to books of accounts, files, safes, security documents and other records and information relating to the accounts, of all federal ministries/extra ministerial offices and other arms of government, or units."

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: If a payment voucher is reported lost, and it is established that no loss or fraud has occurred, to whom should the Accounting Officer submit a report on the circumstances of the loss?
options: The Head of Civil Service. E3
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 630 states: "Where the Accounting Officer is satisfied that no loss or fraud has taken place he will submit a report On the circumstances of the loss of the voucher to the Accountant -General..." Financial Regulations: Bank Accounts and Cheques

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines necessitate for any bank account beyond the three specified types?
options: Approval from the Minister of Finance.
A resolution from the National Assembly.Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Express approval of the Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 701 (ii) states: "No other bank account must be allowed without the express approval of the Accountant-General."

questions.question: In the public service context, which office is permit to issue Imprests?
options: The Accountant-General of the Federation and Accounting Officers of Self-Accounting ministries/extra-ministerialoffices.
The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.3.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General of the Federation and Accounting Officers of Self-Accounting ministries/extra-ministerialoffices.). Financial Regulation 1002 states that "Imprests are issued by the Accountant-General of the Federation and the Accounting Officers of Self Accounting ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: To whom should applications for imprests be made? 227?
options: The Accountant-General or, in the case of a self-accounting agency, to the Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Accountant-General or, in the case of a self-accounting agency, to the Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 1005 directs that "Applications for imprests should be made to the Accountant-General or in the case of a self-accounting agency, to the Accounting Officer..."

questions.question: In official practice, responsibility for has the authority to inspect the accounts of cash advance Holders belongs to which role?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.). Financial Regulation 1013 states: "The accounts of cash advance Holders are subject to inspection by the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General."

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: How often must monthly returns of all receipt and licence books issued, used, and unused be rendered, and to whom?
options: Bi-annually, to the Head of Department.Q.
Annually, to the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Monthly, to the Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1221 mandates "monthly returns of all receipt and licence books issued, used and unused... to the Accountant-General."

questions.question: which role approves the grant of self-accounting status to a Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or other arms of government?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1602 states that the Accountant-General's approval is necessitate for self-accounting status

questions.question: In the public service context, which role has been assigned the accountability for all financial business of the government of the federation by the President?
options: The Head of the Civil Service
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Minister of Finance. Q). Financial Regulation 103 states, "The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria has assigned to the Minister of Finance the accountability for all financial business of the government of the federation." 145

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines the scope of application for the financial regulations issued by the Minister of Finance?
options: The Federal Public Service, including ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of government.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Public Service, including ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of government.). Financial Regulation 105 states, "The financial regulations so issued must generally apply to the Federal Public Service, which means 'ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of government."

questions.question: In the public service context, responsibility for is the Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General of the Federation.). Financial Regulation 106 states, "The Accountant-General of the Federation is the Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury."

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: How does the Accountant-General provide financial guidelines to federal ministries and other arms of government?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (By issuing treasury circulars.). Financial Regulation 107(k) states the Accountant-General must "provide financial guidelines through the issuance of treasury circulars."

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: Question: What is one of the Accountant-General's duties related to revenue?
options: To set revenue targets for ministries.
To carry out revenue monitoring and accounting.
To collect all federal government revenue.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To carry out revenue monitoring and accounting.). Financial Regulation 107(m) states the Accountant-General must "carry out revenue monitoring and accounting."

questions.question: In the public service context, which role is personally and peculiarly accountable for the due performance of the financial duties of their ministries and for any inaccuracies in accounts rendered under their authority?
options: Only the Head of Finance and Accounts. E3
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (All officers.). Financial Regulation 124 states, "All officers are personally and peculiarly in charge for the due performance of the financial duties of their ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."
